title: Romania removes penalties for private same-sex acts
description: Romania amended Article 200 of its penal code in 1996, ending criminal punishment for consensual same-sex acts conducted in private. Public expression remained penalized until the article's full repeal in 2001.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: North Macedonia decriminalizes same-sex acts
description: In 1996 the Republic of Macedonia (today North Macedonia) removed consensual same-sex activity from its criminal code.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Macau decriminalizes same-sex acts
description: Macau's new penal code of 1996, adopted while the territory was under Portuguese administration, dropped criminal sanctions for consensual same-sex activity.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Canada adds sexual orientation to federal human rights law
description: Canada's Parliament amended the Canadian Human Rights Act in 1996 to include sexual orientation as a prohibited ground of discrimination at the federal level.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: US television airs its first lesbian wedding on Friends
description: The sitcom Friends broadcast the first lesbian wedding shown on American television in 1996, marrying the characters Carol and Susan, played by Jane Sibbett and Jessica Hecht.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Steve Fong is the first openly gay speaker at a Republican convention
description: Steve Fong of California, a member of the Log Cabin Republicans, spoke at the 1996 Republican National Convention as its first openly gay speaker.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Muffin Spencer-Devlin comes out as the first openly gay LPGA golfer
description: Professional golfer Muffin Spencer-Devlin came out in 1996, becoming the first LPGA player to do so publicly.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Fiji's constitution bans sexual-orientation discrimination
description: Fiji's 1997 constitution included sexual orientation among the grounds protected against discrimination, one of the earliest constitutional protections of its kind worldwide.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: South Africa's final constitution takes effect with LGBTQ protection
description: South Africa's post-apartheid constitution came into force in February 1997, keeping sexual orientation as an explicitly protected ground in its equality clause — the first national constitution in the world to do so.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Russia equalizes the age of consent
description: Russia's new criminal code, in force from 1997, set the same age of consent for same-sex and different-sex activity.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Ecuador decriminalizes same-sex acts
description: Ecuador's Constitutional Tribunal struck down the criminal ban on consensual same-sex activity in 1997, ending prosecution of gay and lesbian people under the penal code.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Venezuela listed as decriminalizing same-sex acts
description: The Wikipedia timeline dates Venezuela's decriminalization of consensual same-sex activity to 1997. Other sources indicate Venezuela had no national criminal ban in the modern era, so the dating needs verification.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Tasmania becomes the last Australian state to decriminalize
description: In 1997 Tasmania repealed its criminal ban on consensual same-sex activity, the final Australian state to do so, following federal pressure and a UN Human Rights Committee finding.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Israeli president likens homosexuality to alcoholism
description: Speaking to high-school students in 1997, Israeli President Ezer Weizman compared homosexuality to alcoholism, drawing criticism from LGBTQ groups.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: UK grants immigration rights to same-sex partners
description: In 1997 the United Kingdom introduced immigration rules allowing foreign same-sex partners of UK residents to settle on terms comparable to those for married couples.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Ellen DeGeneres comes out, on and off screen
description: In April 1997 Ellen DeGeneres came out publicly as a lesbian, among the first major US celebrities to do so. Her sitcom character Ellen Morgan came out later that year, making her the first openly lesbian actress playing an openly lesbian lead on television.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Patria Jiménez enters the Mexican Congress as its first openly gay member
description: Patria Jiménez won a seat in Mexico's Congress in 1997 for the Party of the Democratic Revolution, the first openly gay person elected to the national legislature.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First peer-reviewed journal on LGBT health launches
description: The Gay and Lesbian Medical Association started the Journal of the Gay and Lesbian Medical Association in 1997, the first peer-reviewed multi-disciplinary journal devoted to LGBT health.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First open-mouth kiss between women on US prime-time TV
description: The ABC drama Relativity aired the first open-mouth kiss between two women shown on American prime-time television in 1997.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Catalonia enacts Spain's first same-sex partnership law
description: The Spanish autonomous community of Catalonia passed a stable-couples law in 1998 that took effect the same year, giving same-sex couples legal recognition for the first time anywhere in Spain.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Ecuador's new constitution bans sexual-orientation discrimination
description: Ecuador adopted a constitution in 1998 that lists sexual orientation as a protected ground against discrimination, the first such constitutional clause in the Americas.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Ireland outlaws employment discrimination based on sexual orientation
description: Ireland's Employment Equality Act of 1998 prohibited workplace discrimination on the ground of sexual orientation.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Prince Edward Island and Alberta gain sexual-orientation protection
description: In 1998 Prince Edward Island added sexual orientation to its human rights legislation, and Canada's Supreme Court ruled in Vriend v. Alberta that Alberta's human rights law must be read as including it; Alberta amended its statute only in 2009.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Matthew Shepard is murdered in Wyoming
description: University student Matthew Shepard was beaten and left to die near Laramie, Wyoming, in October 1998 because he was gay. His murder provoked national outrage and became a catalyst for US hate-crime legislation.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Rita Hester is murdered in Massachusetts
description: Rita Hester, a Black transgender woman, was stabbed to death in her apartment in Allston, Massachusetts, in November 1998. Her killing inspired the founding of the Transgender Day of Remembrance the following year.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Bosnia and Herzegovina decriminalizes same-sex acts
description: Bosnia and Herzegovina removed criminal penalties for consensual same-sex activity in 1998, starting with the Federation entity; Republika Srpska followed in 2000.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Kazakhstan decriminalizes same-sex acts
description: Kazakhstan's post-Soviet criminal code, in force from 1998, dropped the ban on consensual same-sex activity between men.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Kyrgyzstan decriminalizes same-sex acts
description: Kyrgyzstan repealed the Soviet-era criminal ban on consensual same-sex activity between men in 1998.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: South Africa's Constitutional Court strikes down the sodomy law
description: In October 1998 South Africa's Constitutional Court ruled the common-law offence of sodomy unconstitutional, with effect backdated to 1994, ending criminalization of sex between men.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Cyprus decriminalizes same-sex acts
description: The Republic of Cyprus repealed its criminal ban on consensual sex between men in 1998, complying with the European Court of Human Rights judgment in Modinos v. Cyprus.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Tajikistan decriminalizes same-sex acts
description: Tajikistan's revised criminal code of 1998 removed the offence covering consensual same-sex activity between men.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Croatia equalizes the age of consent
description: Croatia set the same age of consent for same-sex and different-sex activity in 1998.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Latvia equalizes the age of consent
description: Latvia adopted an equal age of consent for same-sex and different-sex activity with its new criminal law, passed in 1998; some sources date the change to its entry into force in 1999.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Romania ends its ban on gay people in the military
description: Romania lifted its prohibition on gay and lesbian people serving in the armed forces in 1998.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: South Africa ends its ban on gay people in the military
description: South Africa opened military service to gay and lesbian personnel in 1998, ending the exclusion policy of the apartheid-era defence force.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: PFLAG becomes the first US national LGBT group with a trans-inclusion policy
description: At its 1998 annual meeting in San Francisco, Parents and Friends of Lesbians and Gays voted to add gender identity to its mission, making it the first national LGBT organization in the United States to formally adopt transgender inclusion.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Tammy Baldwin is the first open lesbian elected to the US Congress
description: Tammy Baldwin won Wisconsin's 2nd congressional district in November 1998, becoming the first open lesbian elected to the US Congress and the first openly gay or lesbian candidate to win a seat as a non-incumbent.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Dana International wins Eurovision for Israel
description: Israeli singer Dana International won the Eurovision Song Contest in May 1998 with the song Diva, the first transgender artist to take the title.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Rob Halford comes out as heavy metal's first openly gay star
description: Judas Priest singer Rob Halford came out publicly in 1998, becoming the first openly gay heavy metal musician of prominence.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: The bisexual pride flag debuts
description: The first bisexual pride flag, designed by Michael Page, was presented publicly on 5 December 1998.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: British TV's first transgender character appears on Coronation Street
description: Julie Hesmondhalgh began playing Hayley Anne Patterson on Coronation Street in 1998, the first transgender character on British television.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: BiNet USA holds the First National Institute on Bisexuality and HIV/AIDS
description: BiNet USA convened the First National Institute on Bisexuality and HIV/AIDS in 1998, addressing bisexual health within the epidemic response.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: California creates a statewide domestic-partnership registry
description: California passed a domestic-partnership law in 1999 that took effect the same period, the first statewide partnership registry for same-sex couples in the United States. It initially excluded adoption rights, which were added in later years.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: France introduces the PACS civil partnership
description: France passed the civil solidarity pact (PACS) in November 1999, opening a national civil-partnership status to same-sex couples; the law took effect that same month.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Chile decriminalizes same-sex acts
description: Chile repealed the criminal ban on consensual same-sex activity between adult men in 1999.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Finland equalizes the age of consent
description: Finland set an equal age of consent for same-sex and different-sex activity in 1999 as part of a reform of its sexual-offences law.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Queer Youth Alliance forms in the UK
description: The Queer Youth Alliance, an organization for young LGBTQ people, was established in the United Kingdom in 1999.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Israel's Supreme Court recognizes a lesbian co-mother
description: In 1999 Israel's Supreme Court recognized a woman as a second legal mother of her partner's biological son, a first for same-sex parenting rights in the country.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: South Africa extends spousal immigration benefits to same-sex partners
description: In December 1999 South Africa's Constitutional Court required that foreign same-sex partners of residents receive the immigration benefits available to spouses.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Steven Greenberg comes out as an openly gay Orthodox rabbi
description: Rabbi Steven Greenberg, ordained at Yeshiva University's Orthodox seminary, came out as gay in 1999 in the Israeli newspaper Maariv. He is widely described as the first openly gay Orthodox rabbi, though some Orthodox authorities dispute the label.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Transgender Day of Remembrance is founded
description: Activist Gwendolyn Ann Smith started the Transgender Day of Remembrance in 1999 to memorialize the murder of Rita Hester in Allston, Massachusetts. Observed each year on 20 November, it grew from a web project into an international day of action.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: First Celebrate Bisexuality Day is held
description: Michael Page, Gigi Raven Wilbur and Wendy Curry organized the first Celebrate Bisexuality Day in 1999, observed on 23 September and now marked internationally.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Vermont enacts the first US civil-union law
description: Vermont passed a civil-union law in 2000 that took effect the same year, the first US state to give same-sex couples a legal status with the state-level rights of marriage.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: South Africa passes PEPUDA equality legislation
description: South Africa's Promotion of Equality and Prevention of Unfair Discrimination Act of 2000 prohibited discrimination, hate speech and harassment, covering sexual orientation among other grounds.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Scotland repeals Section 28
description: The Scottish Parliament repealed Section 28 in June 2000, removing the clause that barred local authorities from what the law termed promoting homosexuality — three years before England and Wales.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: UK lifts its ban on gay people in the armed forces
description: In January 2000 the United Kingdom ended its prohibition on lesbian, gay and bisexual personnel serving in the military, following a European Court of Human Rights ruling.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Belarus equalizes the age of consent
description: Belarus is listed as adopting an equal age of consent for same-sex and different-sex activity in 2000; its new criminal code entered into force in 2001, so the exact year needs verification.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Israel equalizes the age of consent
description: Israel set the same age of consent for same-sex and different-sex activity in 2000 through an amendment to its penal law.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: UK passes an equal age of consent
description: The UK Parliament passed the Sexual Offences (Amendment) Act in November 2000, lowering the age of consent for sex between men to match the general age; the change took effect in January 2001.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Azerbaijan decriminalizes same-sex acts
description: Azerbaijan's new criminal code, adopted in 2000, removed the Soviet-era offence covering consensual sex between men.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Georgia decriminalizes same-sex acts
description: Georgia repealed the criminal ban on consensual same-sex activity between men in 2000 with the adoption of its new criminal code.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Bundestag apologizes for Nazi-era and postwar persecution of homosexuals
description: In December 2000 Germany's Bundestag formally apologized to gay men and lesbians persecuted under the Nazi regime and acknowledged the harm done to homosexual citizens up to 1969, the year Paragraph 175 was reformed.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Israel recognizes same-sex partners for immigration
description: In 2000 Israel began recognizing same-sex relationships for immigration purposes, allowing a foreign partner of an Israeli resident to obtain status.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Transgender Pride flag flies for the first time
description: The Transgender Pride flag, created by Monica Helms, was shown publicly for the first time at a pride parade in Phoenix, Arizona, in 2000.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Hillary Clinton is the first First Lady to march in a pride parade
description: In 2000 Hillary Clinton marched in an LGBT pride parade, the first US First Lady to take part in one.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)

title: Jim Kolbe addresses the Republican National Convention
description: Congressman Jim Kolbe of Arizona spoke at the 2000 Republican National Convention, the first openly gay person to address the gathering, though his speech avoided the topic of gay rights.

sources.label: Wikipedia — Timeline of LGBTQ history, 20th century (CC BY-SA 4.0)
